No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Immunology,' 'cancer,' and 'Stem cells,' directly contributing to understanding and combating diseases (Targets 3.3, 3.4) and medical research (Target 3.b).

Quality Education.reason: As a required graduate-level course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops advanced scientific skills for neuroscience research, contributing to relevant learning outcomes (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the syllabus suggests a focus on gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the syllabus links to water quality, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the syllabus links to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The advanced scientific knowledge gained in molecular cell biology and neuroscience research can indirectly contribute to technological upgrading and innovation, supporting higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly enhances scientific research and technological capabilities by training students in molecular cell biology and its application to neuroscience research, aligning with Target 9.5 on innovation.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.
